destinations.evidence: The short-result transition is one-way from AwaitingExecution to Fulfilled; already fulfilled or later two-phase states fail the transition guard.
The regression test exercises the second fulfillment attempt and confirms it is rejected as InvalidState rather than overwriting the fulfilled result.

equivalence_argument: The v2.2 async pool has additional two-phase states, but the execution-result transition still has a single valid source state. submit_result_with_context rejects missing entries and every non-AwaitingExecution state before setting state/result/proof, so fulfilled metadata cannot be overwritten by a later result submission.
notes: Mapped; two-phase states extend the enum without weakening the execution-result guard.
rationale: The new AsyncState enum has AwaitingExecution and Fulfilled, and fulfill_transaction enforces the transition guard before setting result/proof fields. This result keeps the candidate as a distinct refined invariant because the bounded assignment packet does not contain a newer candidate that clearly supersedes it. Implementation-detail caveat: Additional async states could be introduced later, making this a first implementation of the state machine rather than a stable external contract.
statement: A transaction in the async pool may be fulfilled only from AwaitingExecution state; fulfillment sets the state to Fulfilled and stores the output and optional proof, while missing transactions return TransactionNotFound and already fulfilled transactions return InvalidState.
suggested_falsification_tests: Insert an AwaitingExecution async transaction, fulfill it once and verify state/result/proof; then call fulfill again and assert InvalidState, and call fulfill for an unknown hash and assert TransactionNotFound.
summary: Async fulfillment remains a guarded one-way transition from AwaitingExecution to Fulfilled.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Insert an AwaitingExecution async transaction, fulfill it once and verify state/result/proof; then call fulfill again and assert InvalidState, and call fulfill for an unknown hash and assert TransactionNotFound.
title: Async fulfillment is a one-way state transition
